data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube employs a microservices-based architecture allowing for independent development, deployment, and scaling of components.
data.technical_spec: Services are containerized using Docker and orchestrated with Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: YouTube utilizes a CDN to ensure video delivery is fast and efficient globally.
data.technical_spec: Global Edge servers cache content close to users; integrates with Google's global infrastructure.

data.title: Data Storage Infrastructure
data.description: Videos and metadata are stored using distributed systems for scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Google’s Bigtable and Spanner are used for data storage and management.

data.title: Streaming Protocols
data.description: Dynamic video streaming using adaptive bitrate to optimize quality.
data.technical_spec: Uses DASH and HLS protocols for adaptive streaming.

data.title: Scalable API Design
data.description: APIs support various functionalities like video upload, streaming, data retrieval.
data.technical_spec: RESTful design, OAuth 2.0 for secure access.
